context.street_character: One-way EB | 40 km/h | Sub-standard sidewalks | Mixed commercial-residential

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: dark mottled red-brown brick with white-framed modern windows and white porch trim
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Facade appears occupied but visually compromised by accumulated bins, simplified trim, and altered openings.
photo_observations.window_details: Large modern lower windows and a smaller upper window sit within an altered brick frontage.
photo_observations.door_details: Entry is slightly recessed beside the front window and reached by a short run of steps.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Minimal fascia and trim remain visible at the roof edge.
photo_observations.bay_window_details: Historic bay form is no longer clearly legible on this altered front elevation.
photo_observations.porch_details: Short stoop with small landing and simple railing elements.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney visible from this cropped view.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Very little original decoration remains visible due to the altered windows and infill.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Large replacement windows, simplified porch treatment, and facade remodelling obscure the original house-form character.
photo_observations.notes: Observed from a cropped frontage view at the west end of the Nassau Street sequence.

notes: Observed from a cropped frontage view at the west end of the Nassau Street sequence.

facade_detail.composition: The street frontage spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys beneath a cross-gable, consistent with its commercial, converted house-form, semi-detached typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The visible opening pattern is organized as 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Key storefront and facade features include original windows, cornice.

facade_description: Two-storey converted semi-detached house, pre-1889 origins with 1914-1930 alterations. Dark mottled red-brown brick with replacement windows and simplified trim. Asymmetric opening pattern (2 ground, 1 upper). Cross-gable roof, party walls on both sides; 3 dwelling units. Fair condition with overgrown front and altered openings.
